pair: Basal Cell Carcinoma vs Benign Keratosis

rules.pair: Basal Cell Carcinoma vs Benign Keratosis
rules.rule: If arborizing (tree-like) vessels are visible — large, irregularly branching vessels — it is Basal Cell Carcinoma.

rules.pair: Basal Cell Carcinoma vs Benign Keratosis
rules.rule: If blue-gray ovoid nests are visible (large, well-defined, blue-gray oval or round structures distinct from the background), it is Basal Cell Carcinoma.

rules.pair: Basal Cell Carcinoma vs Benign Keratosis
rules.rule: If leaf-like areas are present (discrete brown, gray, or gray-green leaf or petal-shaped structures at the periphery), it is Basal Cell Carcinoma.

rules.pair: Basal Cell Carcinoma vs Benign Keratosis
rules.rule: If spoke-wheel structures are present (well-defined radial projections meeting at a darker central hub), it is Basal Cell Carcinoma.

rules.pair: Basal Cell Carcinoma vs Benign Keratosis
rules.rule: If the surface appears pearly or translucent with a rolled, shiny border, it favors Basal Cell Carcinoma.

rules.pair: Basal Cell Carcinoma vs Benign Keratosis
rules.rule: If ulceration (a well-defined central erosion or crusted area) is present without preceding trauma, it favors Basal Cell Carcinoma.

rules.pair: Basal Cell Carcinoma vs Benign Keratosis
rules.rule: If milia-like cysts are present (multiple white or yellowish round, well-defined dots scattered throughout the lesion), it is Benign Keratosis (seborrheic keratosis).

rules.pair: Basal Cell Carcinoma vs Benign Keratosis
rules.rule: If comedo-like openings are present (dark brown to black, round or irregular plugged follicular openings resembling blackheads), it is Benign Keratosis.

rules.pair: Basal Cell Carcinoma vs Benign Keratosis
rules.rule: If a cerebriform or brain-like pattern is visible (gyri and sulci pattern, thick ridges and fissures resembling the surface of a brain), it is Benign Keratosis.

rules.pair: Basal Cell Carcinoma vs Benign Keratosis
rules.rule: If fissures and ridges form a 'fat finger' or 'sulci and gyri' pattern (thick, parallel ridges separated by deep grooves), it is Benign Keratosis.

rules.pair: Basal Cell Carcinoma vs Benign Keratosis
rules.rule: If the border has a moth-eaten or sharply demarcated edge (concave indentations giving a 'bitten-off' appearance), it favors Benign Keratosis.

rules.pair: Basal Cell Carcinoma vs Benign Keratosis
rules.rule: If hairpin vessels with a surrounding white halo are present, it favors Benign Keratosis (seborrheic keratosis).
